contentDescriptions.description: Investigate the ancient world and describe how the characteristics of ancient societies shaped the development of civilisations.
Explain the causes and effects of significant events in the ancient world and how they shaped subsequent history.
Describe the characteristics of places at different scales and explain how geographic features influence where and how people live.
Investigate the distribution and characteristics of world biomes and explain how climate influences living conditions.
Explain the key features of Australian democracy including the separation of powers, voting rights and the Constitution.
Investigate the medieval world including the significance of the Black Death, feudalism and the role of religion.
Describe the impact of the Crusades on European and Middle Eastern societies.
Investigate the geography of Asia and Australia's connections with Asia.
Investigate the causes, course and consequences of the Industrial Revolution and its impact on Australian colonial history.
Analyse the experience of convicts, free settlers and Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples in colonial Australia.
Explain how colonial Australia developed and the factors that shaped its society, economy and culture.
Investigate the geographic factors that influence the management of Australia's water resources and explain the challenges of water security.
Analyse data to explain spatial patterns and trends in natural hazards and evaluate their impact on communities.
Explain the operation of markets in Australia and evaluate the role of consumers, producers and the government.
Investigate the causes, course and consequences of the First World War and Australia's involvement.
Analyse significant events in Australian history in the 20th century including federation, immigration policy and civil rights movements.
Investigate environmental change and evaluate human responses to climate change in Australia and globally.
